title: Weeknight Chicken Ramen

overview.description: A quick homemade chicken ramen built on rich chicken stock, miso and soy sauce, finished with noodles, chicken and fresh toppings.

method: Bring the chicken stock to a gentle simmer.
Add the ginger, garlic and whole spring onions.
Whisk together the miso, soy sauce and mirin with a ladleful of hot broth until smooth.
Return the mixture to the pan.
Add the mushrooms and pak choi and cook for 3–4 minutes.
Meanwhile, cook the ramen noodles according to the packet instructions.
Warm the sliced chicken in the broth for 2 minutes.
Ladle over the broth, vegetables and chicken.
Top with soft-boiled egg, sliced spring onions and sesame seeds.

learning.did-you-know: Japanese ramen is usually built from three elements: broth, tare (seasoning base) and aroma oil.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How to turn homemade chicken stock into a balanced ramen broth using miso and soy sauce.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: A practical homemade ramen that uses homemade chicken stock while remaining achievable on a weeknight.
chapman-tips: Don't boil the miso after adding it—gentle heat preserves its flavour.
Cook the noodles separately so they don't cloud the broth.
Leftover roast chicken works beautifully in place of freshly cooked chicken.
Serve immediately once the noodles are added.
notes.batch-cooking: Freeze only the broth. Cook fresh noodles when serving.
